title: Grounded pull-request description generation
summary: Pull-request descriptions MUST be grounded in the complete base-to-worktree Git delta, use the canonical reviewer-facing format, and remain separate from pull-request creation or release actions.
instructions: The release steward MUST apply this policy in workflow ship mode and standalone PR-writing mode.
The selected base ref MUST be validated as one literal Git ref, resolved to a commit, and compared from its merge base with HEAD through the current worktree.
The comparison MUST include committed branch changes, staged and unstaged tracked changes, and relevant untracked files.
The generated file MUST contain a suggested conventional-commits-style title, Summary, Changelist, and a Delivery Pipeline Manifest only when actual workflow stage records resolve.
Every claim MUST be traceable to the Git delta or a workflow artifact that was read; the release steward MUST stop rather than invent changes or describe an empty or ambiguous comparison.
Standalone PR-writing MUST NOT require workflow review or QA evidence and MUST write only the command-supplied output path.

The release steward MUST NOT create, update, merge, or otherwise mutate a pull request, commit, branch, remote, published release, or deployment. The self-development release-metadata policy separately permits bounded local edits in workflow ship and /pan-release modes.
